author: jaredrsommer
title: Add A2A Mesh: Local agent network with orchestration and web UI
raw_text:
## Summary

This PR introduces **A2A Mesh**, a complete framework for building local multi-device agent networks with master-slave orchestration, intent-based approval workflows, networked file sharing, and a web UI dashboard.

## Key Changes

### Core Agent Framework (Phase 1)
- **`mesh/agent/config.py`**: YAML-based configuration loader supporting OpenAI-compatible LLM endpoints (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio, etc.)
- **`mesh/agent/base.py`**: Unified `LLMClient` abstraction using AsyncOpenAI for any OpenAI-compatible backend
- **`mesh/agent/executor.py`**: A2A `AgentExecutor` implementation bridging the A2A protocol with LLM chat completions
- **`mesh/agent/tools.py`**: Tool/function calling registry for exposing capabilities to LLMs

### Network & Discovery (Phase 2)
- **`mesh/network/discovery.py`**: mDNS/DNS-SD service announcement and discovery for auto-discovering master nodes
- **`mesh/network/registry.py`**: Master-hosted agent registry with REST API and heartbeat monitoring
- **`mesh/network/topology.py`**: Star topology manager handling agent registration, discovery, and health checks
- **`mesh/network/models.py`**: Data models for nodes and network information

### Master-Slave Orchestration (Phase 3)
- **`mesh/orchestrator/master.py`**: Master orchestrator agent that decomposes tasks, routes them to agents, and reviews intentions
- **`mesh/orchestrator/intent.py`**: Intent declaration, review, and approval system with risk levels and approval policies
- **`mesh/orchestrator/router.py`**: Skill-based task routing to find the best agent for each task
- **`mesh/orchestrator/autonomy.py`**: Autonomous execution engine with configurable autonomy levels and guardrails

### Networked File Share (Phase 4)
- **`mesh/fileshare/server.py`**: HTTP-based file server hosted on master node for shared workspace access
- **`mesh/fileshare/client.py`**: File operations client for agents to read/write shared files

### Web UI (Phase 5)
- **`mesh/ui/api.py`**: REST API backend with endpoints for cluster status, agents, chat, and approval workflows
- **`mesh/ui/ws.py`**: WebSocket server for real-time dashboard updates

### External Tool Integration (Phase 6)
- **`mesh/integrations/generic_cli.py`**: Base class for wrapping CLI tools as A2A agents
- **`mesh/integrations/claude_code.py`**: Claude Code CLI wrapper
- **`mesh/integrations/aider.py`**: Aider CLI wrapper

### Server & CLI
- **`mesh/server.py`**: ASGI app factory for creating master or slave node servers
- **`mesh/cli.py`**: CLI entry point with `mesh start agent --config` command

### Configuration & Documentation
- **`configs/`**: Example YAML configurations for master, researcher, coder, reviewer, and Claude Code agents
- **`plans/a2a-mesh/docs/`**: Comprehensive phase-by-phase documentation (6 phases)
- **`plans/a2a-mesh/PLAN.md`**: High-level vision and architecture overview
- **`docs/research/`**: Research document on OpenAI compatibility and local mesh feasibility
- **`pyproject.toml`**: Project metadata and dependencies

## Notable Implementation Details

- **Intent-based approval workflow**: Agents declare intentions before taking significant actions; master reviews based on configurable risk levels and approval policies
- **OpenAI-compatible LLM support**: Works with any endpoint exposing `/v1/chat/completions` (Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio, OpenAI, etc.)
- **Star network topology**: Single master node with multiple slave agents; auto-discovery via mDNS eliminates manual configuration
- **Shared workspace**: HTTP-based file server allows agents across devices to collaborate on files
- **Autonomous execution with guardrails**: Configurable autonomy levels (manual, supervised,

https://claude.ai/code/session_01SSeXjf2zEYFacn62zNjcoK

author: mikeas1
title: docs: Expand details on In-Task Authorization.
raw_text:
The previous wording was very barebones. We have explored this area significantly more, and the specification now reflects further guidance on how to handle retrieving authorizations and credentials during Task processing.

This specifically addresses the missing guidance on how clients are expected to respond to a Task in `TASK_STATE_AUTH_REQUIRED`.

Fixes #1582.

author: ibrahimokuyucu
title: feat(extensions): add AIAR identity extension (v0 MVP)
raw_text:
## Summary

This PR introduces the **Agent Identity and Accountability Reporting (AIAR)** extension (`v0` MVP), a backwards-compatible A2A extension that provides verifiable identity binding for Agent Cards and runtime proof-of-possession for agent endpoints.

**Problem:** The A2A protocol enables agent discovery and communication but does not provide a mechanism for clients to verify that an Agent Card or runtime endpoint is bound to a verifiable issuer identity. Discovery is not identity; runtime auth is not ownership.

**Solution:** AIAR adds opt-in, cryptographic identity primitives using standard formats (JWS, JCS canonicalization) that higher-level trust frameworks can build upon.

### What's included

- **Extension specification** (`docs/extensions/aiar-identity-v0.md`):
  - Agent Card identity params: `issuer`, `kid`, `signedFields`, `jws`, `attestations`
  - Signing target definition using RFC 8785 (JCS) canonicalization
  - Step-by-step verification procedure
  - Message-level opt-in request (`minAssurance`, `acceptableIssuers`, `requireFreshnessSeconds`)
  - Response proof bundle (`cardDigest`, `proofOfPossession`, timestamps)
  - Optional RPC methods: `aiar.getProofBundle`, `aiar.getIssuer`
  - Security considerations (Sybil, replay, key compromise, phishing, privacy)

- **Specification updates** (`docs/specification.md`):
  - AIAR Agent Card example in Section 4.6 (Extension Declaration)
  - Message-level identity request example

- **Extensions list update** (`docs/topics/extensions.md`):
  - AIAR added to the extension list table

- **Example payloads** (`examples/aiar-identity/`):
  - `agent-card.json` — standard Agent Card without AIAR
  - `agent-card.signed.json` — Agent Card with AIAR extension params + JWS
  - `message.request.json` — SendMessage with identity requirements in metadata
  - `message.response.json` — task response with proof bundle in metadata
  - `proof-bundle.json` — standalone proof bundle (as returned by `aiar.getProofBundle`)
  - `README.md` — step-by-step verification guide

### How clients opt in

1. Inspect `capabilities.extensions[]` in the Agent Card for the AIAR URI
2. Include `A2A-Extensions: https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A/extensions/aiar.identity/v0` header
3. Optionally include identity requirements in `SendMessageRequest.metadata`
4. Verify the proof bundle in the response metadata

### What's explicitly out of scope (v0)

- Global governance or federated identity registries
- Certificate revocation infrastructure (CRL/OCSP)
- Reputation systems or behavioral trust metrics
- End-to-end message encryption
- Agent-to-agent mutual authentication
- Automated key rotation (`aiar.rotateKeys` deferred to future version)

### Compatibility

- **No breaking changes**: all AIAR data uses existing extension points (`AgentExtension.params`, `Message.metadata`, `Task.metadata`)
- **No core schema modifications**: no changes to `a2a.proto` or core data structures
- **Graceful degradation**: agents/clients unaware of AIAR are completely unaffected

### Checklist

- [x] `docs/extensions/aiar-identity-v0.md` exists with full spec + examples
- [x] Agent Card schema/docs updated to show extension declaration + AIAR example
- [x] Message schema/docs updated with AIAR request + proof bundle response
- [x] Optional RPC methods defined in spec
- [x] `examples/` folder includes valid JSON payloads + verification README
- [x] No breaking changes; existing schemas remain valid

author: herczyn
title: fix(docs): Clearer wording around context id.
raw_text:
client side context id passing can be supported, server can choose to reject, client's should know how to fallback accordingly
if server accepts, server must respond with the context id
if server cannot accept client side context id it MUST respond with an error (i.e. it SHOULD not respond with a task with a different context id?)
servers may not accept a client side context id for a number of reasons: clashes, validation errors, not supporting context ids

author: vinoo999
title: docs(spec)!: make contextId fully optional on server side
raw_text:
BREAKING CHANGE: Task.context_id is now OPTIONAL in a2a.proto (previously [REQUIRED](https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A/blob/d1ed0da587d2d634ba0b81a40d082cee0850b81b/specification/a2a.proto)). Agents MAY generate a new contextId and include it in the response, but are no longer required to do so.

Clients MAY provide a contextId in a Message. Agents MUST accept and preserve client-provided contextId values if validations pass as before.

This aligns more concretely with TSC direction in #1581 that contextId should be optional as the agent need not provide a tying identifier.

Fixes #1581

author: vinoo999
title: docs(spec): clarify contextId optional generation and client side semantics
raw_text:
Clients MAY provide a contextId in a Message to associate it with an existing context. Server MUST generate contextId when creating a Task but MAY omit it when responding with a Message only (no task created).

Keeps Task.context_id as REQUIRED in [a2a.proto](https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A/blob/d1ed0da587d2d634ba0b81a40d082cee0850b81b/specification/a2a.proto) — no breaking change.

Fixes #1581

author: knuckles-stack
title: docs: add authorization, delegation & audit evidence extension proposal
raw_text:
Addresses outstanding work items on authorization schemes (#19, #71) and
capability-based authorization (#1404).

## Summary

This proposal adds three interconnected capabilities to the A2A specification
as an extension:

- **Authorization framework** — Capability-based permission model using the A2A
  extension mechanism, replacing ambient authority with explicit, verifiable
  scopes and a hierarchical scope grammar
- **Delegation chains** — Multi-hop delegation with cryptographic proofs, scope
  narrowing, temporal bounds, chain continuity verification, and revocation
  propagation
- **Audit evidence** — Normative requirements for append-only, tamper-evident
  action records with hash-chained integrity, enabling independent third-party
  verification

Additionally includes a penalty-based trust scoring model, three normative
authorization flows (direct, delegated, trust-gated), worked examples
(financial services, healthcare, supply chain), and security considerations
including post-quantum signature readiness.

## Design Decisions

- **Extension, not core change.** All capabilities are defined as extension
  parameters using the existing A2A extension mechanism. No core data
  structures are modified. Existing implementations continue to work.
- **Incremental adoption.** Authorization, delegation, and evidence can each
  be adopted independently.
- **Algorithm agility.** Cryptographic algorithms are parameters (`Ed25519`,
  `ES256`, `ML-DSA-87`), not hardcoded choices.
- **Fail-closed defaults.** Verification failures result in denial.
  Evidence write failures trigger action suspension.

## Motivation

- EU AI Act Article 50 transparency requirements (enforcement August 2, 2026)
- NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative findings on authorization gaps
- CSA survey: 88% of orgs report agent permission incidents, 21% have
  visibility into multi-agent permission chains

## Compatibility

- Fully backward-compatible (extension parameters only)
- Complements existing OAuth 2.0 / API key / OIDC authentication
- Uses standard `A2A-Extensions` header activation
- All pseudocode is language-agnostic

## File

- `docs/topics/authorization-delegation-evidence.md` (~4,200 words)

Closes #19, closes #71, closes #1404

author: dependabot[bot]
title: chore(deps): bump astral-sh/setup-uv from 5 to 7 in the github-actions group
raw_text:
Bumps the github-actions group with 1 update: [astral-sh/setup-uv](https://github.com/astral-sh/setup-uv). Updates `astral-sh/setup-uv` from 5 to 7
Release notes

Sourced from astral-sh/setup-uv's releases.

v7.2.1 🌈 update known checksums up to 0.9.28

Changes

🧰 Maintenance

  • chore: update known checksums for 0.9.28 @github-actions[bot] (#744)
  • chore: update known checksums for 0.9.27 @github-actions[bot] (#742)
  • chore: update known checksums for 0.9.26 @github-actions[bot] (#734)
  • chore: update known checksums for 0.9.25 @github-actions[bot] (#733)
  • chore: update known checksums for 0.9.24 @github-actions[bot] (#730)

📚 Documentation

  • Clarify impact of using actions/setup-python @​eifinger (#732)

⬆️ Dependency updates

  • Bump zizmorcore/zizmor-action from 0.3.0 to 0.4.1 @dependabot[bot] (#741)

v7.0.0 🌈 node24 and a lot of bugfixes

Changes

This release comes with a load of bug fixes and a speed up. Because of switching from node20 to node24 it is also a breaking change. If you are running on GitHub hosted runners this will just work, if you are using self-hosted runners make sure, that your runners are up to date. If you followed the normal installation instructions your self-hosted runner will keep itself updated.

This release also removes the deprecated input server-url which was used to download uv releases from a different server. The manifest-file input supersedes that functionality by adding a flexible way to define available versions and where they should be downloaded from.

Fixes

  • The action now respects when the environment variable UV_CACHE_DIR is already set and does not overwrite it. It now also finds cache-dir settings in config files if you set them.
  • Some users encountered problems that cache pruning took forever because they had some uv processes running in the background. Starting with uv version 0.8.24 this action uses uv cache prune --ci --force to ignore the running processes
  • If you just want to install uv but not have it available in path, this action now respects UV_NO_MODIFY_PATH
  • Some other actions also set the env var UV_CACHE_DIR. This action can now deal with that but as this could lead to unwanted behavior in some edgecases a warning is now displayed.

Improvements

If you are using minimum version specifiers for the version of uv to install for example

[tool.uv]
required-version = ">=0.8.17"

This action now detects that and directly uses the latest version. Previously it would download all available releases from the uv repo to determine the highest matching candidate for the version specifier, which took much more time.

If you are using other specifiers like 0.8.x this action still needs to download all available releases because the specifier defines an upper bound (not 0.9.0 or later) and "latest" would possibly not satisfy that.

🚨 Breaking changes

author: agenttrust
title: docs: add AgentTrust to partners list
raw_text:
This PR adds [AgentTrust](https://agenttrust.ai) to the partners list, alphabetically between AG2AI and AI21 Labs.

**About AgentTrust:**
AgentTrust is trust and identity infrastructure for agentic collaboration. We provide hosted A2A endpoints with Agent Card discovery, Ed25519 message signing, human-in-the-loop escalation, and prompt injection detection.

**What we've built:**
- Hosted A2A endpoints — agents get a public A2A identity at `/a2a/{slug}` without running a server
- Standard Agent Card served at `/.well-known/agent.json` per agent
- Direct A2A JSON-RPC ingress (`tasks/send`) with allowlist, InjectionGuard, and HITL rules
- Ed25519 message signing — cryptographic identity verification between agents
- Negotiation lifecycle extending A2A task states via metadata extensions (`propose_complete`, `disputed`, `rejected`)
- MCP server with 13 tools — [npm](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@agenttrust/mcp-server) | [GitHub](https://github.com/agenttrust/mcp-server)
- Webhook delivery with HMAC-SHA256 signatures

**Links:**
- Website: https://agenttrust.ai
- MCP Server: https://github.com/agenttrust/mcp-server
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@agenttrust/mcp-server

Built on Google's A2A protocol. We fill the identity and trust gap — A2A defines how agents talk, AgentTrust helps them prove who they are.

author: holtskinner
title: docs: Fix embedded version of `AgentExtension` in extensions

author: HungYangChang
title: feat!: Proposal for bidirectional streaming over gRPC
raw_text:
# Follow on PR #1120 - BiDi Streaming Discussion Responses

**PR**: [feat!: Proposal for bidirectional streaming over gRPC](https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A/pull/1120)
**Original Author**: mikeas1| **Author**: hungyangchang| **Status**: Open, `TSC Review`

## Summary

Adds `SendLiveMessage` bidirectional streaming RPC to A2A gRPC. Clients maintain an active stream with agents instead of separate follow-up requests. Benefits: inline await, less client state tracking, simpler distributed routing.

## Upstream File Structure Changes

The original PR modified 3 files that no longer exist in upstream `main`:

| File | Old (PR #1120) | Current upstream | Action |
|------|---------------|-----------------|--------|
| `specification/grpc/a2a.proto` | Modified | Moved to `specification/a2a.proto` | Changes applied to new location |
| `specification/json/a2a.json` | Modified | Auto-generated from proto, not in git | None needed |
| `types/src/types.ts` | Modified | Removed entirely | None needed |

`a2a.proto` is now the **single source of truth**. The JSON schema is generated at build time via `scripts/proto_to_json_schema.sh`, and `docs/specification.md` uses `proto_to_table()` macros that auto-render from proto definitions.

---

## Responses

### 1. gemini-code-assist (bot) - Oct 1, 2025

**Field ordering**: Fixed. `bidi_streaming` now follows `extensions` by field number. In new schema it's field 5 (field 4 = `extended_agent_card`).

**`FAILED_PRECONDITION` vs `UNIMPLEMENTED`**: Both are used correctly for distinct cases. `FAILED_PRECONDITION` = duplicate active stream; `UNIMPLEMENTED` = method not supported.

**Omitted `SendMessageConfiguration`**: Already specified: "If omitted, the agent MUST continue to use the configuration from the initial request."

### 2. ToddSegal - Oct 7, 2025

**Resubscribe interaction**: Continues as-is. `SendLiveMessage` is a separate RPC.

**Multiple client calls**: Three rules (per mikeas1):

1. No multi-connection support -> `FAILED_PRECONDITION`
2. Multi-connection support -> treat as resubscribe with multiplexed input
3. Agent has full authority over task lifecycle

**Missing task_id**: Empty values are interpreted as the established stream's task_id/context_id.

### 3. HungYangChang - Dec 11, 2025

**Interrupted vs Failed**: These are distinct states with different BiDi behavior:

| State Type | States | Stream behavior | Resumable? |
|-----------|--------|----------------|------------|
| Interrupted | `INPUT_REQUIRED`, `AUTH_REQUIRED` | Stream stays **open** | Yes, client sends new message on same stream |
| Terminal | `COMPLETED`, `FAILED`, `CANCELED`, `REJECTED` | Agent **half-closes** stream | No |

This is the key difference from `SendStreamingMessage`, which closes on **both** interrupted and terminal states (per [spec 11.1](docs/specification.md): "until the task reaches a terminal or interrupted state, at which point the stream closes").

**State transitions on BiDi stream**: When a Task reaches `INPUT_REQUIRED`:

```
Agent: TaskStatusUpdateEvent (state=INPUT_REQUIRED, message="What is your budget?")
  -- stream stays open, agent awaits inline --
Client: SendMessageRequest (new message with response)
Agent: TaskStatusUpdateEvent (state=WORKING)
  ... processing continues ...
Agent: TaskStatusUpdateEvent (state=COMPLETED) -> half-close
```

The agent can "await" the response inline without saving/reconstituting state. This is the primary advantage over `SendStreamingMessage` + separate `SendMessage` follow-up.

**Timeout**: Not specified in the protocol. Left to agent implementation, consistent with A2A's approach of leaving operational policies to implementations. Options:
- Agent-side idle timeout -> transition to `FAILED` or `CANCELED`
- Future extension: optional `timeout` field in `SendMessageConfiguration`

**Multiple interruptions**: Fully supported. Each interruption keeps the stream open:

```
Client: SendLiveMessage("book a flight to Paris")
Agent: WORKING
Agent: INPUT_REQUIRED ("What is your budget?")
Client: "$500"
Agent: WORKING
Agent: INPUT_REQUIRED ("Confirm date: March 15?")
Client: "Yes"
Agent: WORKING
Agent: COMPLETED (booking artifact) -> half-close
```

**Error codes**:

| Code | Condition |
|------|-----------|
| `UNIMPLEMENTED` | Method not supported |
| `NOT_FOUND` | Unknown task_id |
| `FAILED_PRECONDITION` | Duplicate active stream |
| `INVALID_ARGUMENT` | Malformed request |

### 4. Tehsmash - Feb 25, 2026

**Concern**: BiDi might encourage multi-turn tied to a single Task instead of context-based reuse.

**Response**: This concern is partially valid, and we should be clear about scope:

1. **The BiDi stream IS scoped to a single Task.** The spec states: "the established stream Task" (singular), and "The agent MUST half-close the connection when the Task reaches a terminal state." Once a task completes, the stream closes. You cannot create Task B on the same stream after Task A completes.

2. **BiDi is NOT for multi-turn conversation.** The recommended pattern for multi-turn remains:
   ```
   SendMessage -> Task A -> Complete
   SendMessage (reuse contextId) -> Task B -> Complete
   ```
   This is unchanged. BiDi doesn't replace or conflict with this pattern.

3. **BiDi IS for single-task human-in-the-loop.** The specific use case is tasks that need clarification or approval mid-execution:
   ```
   SendLiveMessage -> Task -> WORKING -> INPUT_REQUIRED (approval needed)
   Client responds on same stream -> WORKING -> COMPLETED -> half-close
   ```
   Without BiDi, this requires: `SendStreamingMessage` -> stream closes on `INPUT_REQUIRED` -> new `SendMessage` -> new `SendStreamingMessage` to get updates. BiDi simplifies this to a single persistent connection.

4. **The "bad" pattern Tehsmash described isn't really bad for BiDi.** The pattern `Task -> input-required -> respond -> input-required -> respond -> complete` is exactly the intended use case. It represents a single unit of work (e.g., booking a flight) that requires multiple human confirmations. This is different from multi-turn conversation where each turn is a separate task.

**Key distinction to document**: BiDi streaming is for **single-task execution with inline interruptions**, not for **multi-task conversational flows**.

---

## Recommended BiDi Streaming Patterns

### When to Use `SendLiveMessage` (BiDi)

Use BiDi streaming when **all** of these apply:
- gRPC transport (BiDi is gRPC-only, not available over JSON-RPC/HTTP)
- Agent declares `capabilities.bidiStreaming: true`
- Task is likely to enter `INPUT_REQUIRED` or `AUTH_REQUIRED` states
- Client wants to respond to interruptions without reconnecting

### When NOT to Use `SendLiveMessage`

- **Simple request/response**: Use `SendMessage` instead
- **One-way streaming** (progress updates only): Use `SendStreamingMessage`
- **Multi-turn conversation** (multiple tasks): Use separate `SendMessage` calls with shared `contextId`
- **Long-running background tasks**: Use `SendMessage` + push notifications
- **Non-gRPC transports**: BiDi is not available over JSON-RPC or HTTP+JSON

### Pattern 1: Single-Task with Human Approval (Recommended BiDi Use Case)

A task that requires one or more human confirmations before completing.

```
┌─────────┐                              ┌─────────┐
│  Client  │                              │  Agent   │
└────┬─────┘                              └────┬─────┘
     │  SendLiveMessage(stream)                │
     │  "Book a flight to Paris"               │
     ├────────────────────────────────────────►│
     │                                         │
     │         Task (id=T1, SUBMITTED)         │
     │◄────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │     StatusUpdate (WORKING)              │
     │◄────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │     StatusUpdate (INPUT_REQUIRED)       │
     │     "Budget? Economy or business?"      │
     │◄────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │                                         │
     │  -- stream stays open --                │
     │                                         │
     │  "Economy, under $500"                  │
     ├────────────────────────────────────────►│
     │                                         │
     │     StatusUpdate (WORKING)              │
     │◄────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │     StatusUpdate (INPUT_REQUIRED)       │
     │     "Found LAX->HEL $450. Confirm?"    │
     │◄────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │                                         │
     │  "Yes, book it"                         │
     ├────────────────────────────────────────►│
     │                                         │
     │     StatusUpdate (WORKING)              │
     │◄────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │     ArtifactUpdate (booking receipt)    │
     │◄────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │     StatusUpdate (COMPLETED)            │
     │◄────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │         --- half-close ---              │
```

### Pattern 2: Multi-Turn Conversation (Do NOT Use BiDi)

For multi-turn conversations, use separate RPC calls with shared `contextId`:

```
┌─────────┐                              ┌─────────┐
│  Client  │                              │  Agent   │
└────┬─────┘                              └────┬─────┘
     │  SendMessage (contextId=C1)             │
     │  "What flights go to Paris?"            │
     ├────────────────────────────────────────►│
     │  Task A (COMPLETED) - flight list       │
     │◄────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │                                         │
     │  SendMessage (contextId=C1)             │
     │  "Book the 2pm Air France one"          │
     ├────────────────────────────────────────►│
     │  Task B (COMPLETED) - booking           │
     │◄────────────────────────────────────────┤
     │                                         │
     │  SendMessage (contextId=C1)             │
     │  "Now find a hotel near CDG"            │
     ├────────────────────────────────────────►│
     │  Task C (COMPLETED) - hotel options     │
     │◄────────────────────────────────────────┤
```

### Pattern 3: Reconnecting to an Active Task

A client can reconnect to an ongoing task's BiDi stream (e.g., after network disruption):

```
Client: SendLiveMessage(task_id=T1, empty parts)
  If T1 is active   -> Agent sends current state + begins updates
  If T1 is terminal  -> Agent sends final Task + half-close
  If T1 not found    -> NOT_FOUND error
  If T1 has existing stream and agent doesn't support multiple -> FAILED_PRECONDITION
```

### Comparison Table

| Scenario | RPC | Transport | Stream closes on |
|----------|-----|-----------|-----------------|
| Simple request/response | `SendMessage` | Any | N/A (unary) |
| Progress updates | `SendStreamingMessage` | Any | Terminal OR interrupted |
| Human-in-the-loop task | **`SendLiveMessage`** | **gRPC only** | **Terminal only** |
| Background task | `SendMessage` + push notifications | Any | N/A |
| Multi-turn conversation | Multiple `SendMessage` calls | Any | N/A |

---

## Changes Made

### Proto (`specification/a2a.proto`)

- Added `SendLiveMessage` bidirectional streaming RPC after `SendStreamingMessage`
- Added `optional bool bidi_streaming = 5` to `AgentCapabilities`
- Removed re-created legacy files (`specification/grpc/a2a.proto`, `specification/json/a2a.json`, `types/src/types.ts`)

### Specification (`docs/specification.md`)

- Added Section 3.1.3: Send Live Message operation (inputs, outputs, errors, behavior, config semantics, reconnection)
- Added `bidiStreaming` capability validation bullet in Section 3.3.4
- Added `SendLiveMessage` row to method mapping table in Section 5.3 (gRPC only, N/A for JSON-RPC and REST)
- Added gRPC method subsection 10.4.3 for `SendLiveMessage`
- Updated Section 10.7 (gRPC Streaming) to cover both server-streaming and bidirectional-streaming
- Added `SendLiveMessage` to operations layer Mermaid diagram

### Topic Docs

- `docs/topics/streaming-and-async.md`: Added "Bidirectional Streaming with gRPC" section with SSE vs BiDi comparison table, usage guidance, and when to/not to use
- `docs/topics/key-concepts.md`: Added bidirectional streaming as 4th interaction mechanism

### Reference Docs

- `docs/llms.txt`: Added `SendLiveMessage` to RPC methods table, `bidi_streaming` to capabilities list
- `docs/whats-new-v1.md`: Added `bidiStreaming` to AgentCapabilities changes, added "NEW: SendLiveMessage" operation section

### Bug Fix

- `docs/topics/extensions.md`: Fixed broken snippet include — `types/src/types.ts:AgentExtension` (deleted file) replaced with `specification/a2a.proto:AgentExtension`

---

## Action Items

- [x] Fix field ordering in `AgentCapabilities`
- [x] Apply changes to `specification/a2a.proto` (new file location)
- [x] Remove re-created legacy files
- [x] Add documentation on recommended BiDi streaming patterns
- [x] Update specification.md (operation, capability validation, method mapping, gRPC section)
- [x] Update streaming-and-async.md, key-concepts.md, llms.txt, whats-new-v1.md
- [x] Fix broken extensions.md snippet include
- [ ] Request TSC review for merge
- [ ] Consider specifying timeout behavior

author: The-Nexus-Guard
title: docs: DID-based agent identity verification extension via AIP
raw_text:
## Summary

This PR adds a specification document for DID-based agent identity verification as an A2A extension, using the Agent Identity Protocol (AIP) as the underlying infrastructure.

## Relationship to PR #1496

This is a **companion** to @abdelsfane's [Agent Identity and Trust Framework](https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A/pull/1496). That PR's design rationale identifies three options:

- **Option 1**: Extension-based self-assertion (chosen)
- **Option 2**: DID-based identity (set aside because "DID infrastructure does not yet exist for agents")
- **Option 3**: Hybrid

This PR provides Option 2 — the DID infrastructure now exists via AIP:

- **Live service**: [aip-service.fly.dev](https://aip-service.fly.dev/docs) (39 endpoints)
- **Python client**: `pip install aip-identity` (288 tests)
- **MCP integration**: `pip install aip-mcp-server` (8 identity tools)

## What's in the spec

1. **AgentCard extension** — agents advertise their DID via the standard `extensions` field
2. **Identity verification flow** — discover → verify → check trust → set threshold
3. **Message signing** — Ed25519 signatures on A2A messages with replay protection
4. **Vouch chains for delegation** — transitive trust with scope narrowing
5. **Real-time revocation** — no CRL distribution delay
6. **Compatibility mapping** — how AIP maps to #1496's Level 0/1/2 model

## Why this matters

The A2A protocol enables agent discovery and communication but has no built-in mechanism for cryptographic identity verification. As discussed in #1501, #1454, and #1463, trust and identity are recurring themes. This spec provides a concrete, working implementation path.

Happy to iterate on the spec based on feedback. The goal is to complement existing proposals, not compete with them.

author: ishymko
title: refactor(spec): rename `SendMessageConfiguration.blocking` to `SendMessageConfiguration.return_immediately `
raw_text:
As mentioned in the linked issue, defaulting to non-blocking behavior is counterintuitive and is implemented the opposite way in the SDKs.

Renaming it to `return_immediately` makes default behavior simpler while advanced scenario requires an extra flag.

---
Previos proposals:
1. `polling` has inaccurate meaning in this context as pointed out in the comments.
2. `non_blocking` is a negative boolean which leads to confusing conditional expressions (`not non_blocking`).

Fixes #1453

author: pinchwork
title: docs: add Pinchwork to partners list
raw_text:
Hi Amye and team,

Following up on issue #1443 ("Add Pinchwork as A2A-compatible agent marketplace") — thank you for the warm welcome and for pointing us to the partners list!

This PR adds [Pinchwork](https://pinchwork.dev) to the partners list, alphabetically between Personal AI and Poppulo.

**About Pinchwork:**
Pinchwork is an agent-to-agent task marketplace where AI agents can post tasks, pick up work, and earn credits — all via a simple API. We have a live A2A endpoint at `/.well-known/agent-card.json` and a JSON-RPC A2A endpoint at `/a2a`, supporting `message/send`, `tasks/get`, and `tasks/cancel`.

We're excited to be part of the A2A ecosystem and look forward to contributing to interoperability between agents.

Resolves #1443

author: thebenignhacker
title: docs: add agent identity verification and trust framework
raw_text:
Closes #1497

## Summary

- Proposes an Agent Identity and Trust Framework for A2A using existing extension points (zero protobuf schema changes)
- Addresses security gaps exploitable in current deployments: agent impersonation, missing trust evaluation, delegation chain attacks, and absent revocation mechanisms
- Defines three identity verification levels (self-asserted, domain-verified, organization-verified), standardized trust signals, mandatory AgentCard signing for production, revocation endpoints, delegation chain security with scope narrowing and depth limits, and application-layer message signing with replay protection

## Files

- `docs/proposals/agent-identity-trust-framework.md` — Main proposal
- `docs/proposals/agent-identity-trust-framework-design-rationale.md` — Design rationale (ADR format, 4 options considered)

## Design Approach

Uses the existing `AgentExtension` mechanism and message `metadata` fields. All changes are additive. Existing agents continue to work at Level 0 (self-asserted). Progressive adoption path from Level 0 to Level 2.

Considered and rejected: W3C DIDs/VCs (too complex for current ecosystem), X.509 certificates (too heavyweight for agent interactions), do-nothing (unacceptable given exploitable gaps).

## Backward Compatibility

No protobuf schema changes. No existing fields modified. The MAY-to-MUST change for AgentCard signing applies only to production deployments over public networks, aligning with the existing MUST for encrypted communication (Section 7.1).

In v0.3 almost all requests had metadata. Event push-related RPCs were extending TaskIDParams inheriting the metadata field from them.

In v1.0 only SendMessage request has it. The absence of metadata is especially problematic in CancelTaskRequest because in some sense it reaches the agent. author: ritsuki1227
title: docs(spec): Add HTTP caching guidance for Agent Card endpoints
raw_text:
Closes #1478. Related to #306.

The specification provides caching guidance for Extended Agent Cards (Sections 3.1.11 and 13.3) but has no equivalent guidance for public Agent Cards. This creates a gap for SDK and framework implementers — none of the five official SDKs currently implement Agent Card caching, and framework implementers have noted the lack of protocol-level guidance (google/adk-python#3297).

This PR adds SHOULD-level HTTP caching guidance, following the precedent of OpenID Connect Discovery 1.0 (same `.well-known` URI pattern).

**Changes:**

- **`docs/specification.md`**: Add Section 8.6 "Caching" with normative guidance on `Cache-Control`, `ETag`, and conditional requests. Placed after Section 8.5 (Sample Agent Card) to avoid renumbering existing sections and breaking anchor links.
- **`docs/topics/agent-discovery.md`**: Add "Caching Considerations" section with practical guidance for implementers.

author: holtskinner
title: docs(spec): Update inconsistencies in documentation for 1.0 Spec
raw_text:
In the future, we should probably explore refactoring the docs set to avoid these becoming out of sync going forward

This pull request updates the project documentation to align with the v1.0 Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol Specification defined in
  specification/a2a.proto. It ensures that all conceptual guides, summaries, and tutorials reflect the current normative definitions,
  RPC method names, and wire formats.

  Summary of Changes


1. RPC Method Naming Updates
  Standardized all method references to use the v1.0 PascalCase naming convention:

   * `message/send` → `SendMessage`
   * `message/stream` → `SendStreamingMessage`
   * `tasks/get` → `GetTask`
   * `tasks/list` → `ListTasks`
   * `tasks/cancel` → `CancelTask`
   * `tasks/pushNotificationConfig/set` → `CreateTaskPushNotificationConfig`
   * `tasks/resubscribe` → `SubscribeToTask`
   * `agent/getAuthenticatedExtendedCard` → `GetExtendedAgentCard`

2. JSON Example & Wire Format Corrections
  Updated examples across all "Topic" files and the Python Quickstart to match v1.0 breaking changes:
   * Removed `kind` discriminators: Examples for Message, Task, Part, and StreamResponse now use member-based polymorphism (e.g.,
     {"text": "..."} instead of {"kind": "text", "text": "..."}).
   * Enum Standardization: Changed role and state enums from kebab-case/lowercase to SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE with type prefixes (e.g.,
     `"ROLE_USER"`, `"TASK_STATE_COMPLETED"`).
   * Response Wrapping: Updated examples to reflect that `SendMessage` and `SendStreamingMessage` return a unified response object where
     the message or task is nested within the result.

  3. LLM Context (`docs/llms.txt`)
   * Completely refreshed the protocol summary and RPC method list to ensure LLM-based tools (like Gemini CLI) have an accurate
     representation of the v1.0 API surface and error codes.

  4. Link & Anchor Maintenance
   * Updated internal documentation links and spans to point to the correct sections in the specification.md (e.g., correcting the
     anchor for GetExtendedAgentCard).

  Why this is needed
  The v1.0 release introduced significant structural improvements for ProtoJSON compliance and architectural consistency. Documentation
  examples were previously showing v0.3.0 patterns which would cause integration errors for developers using the latest SDKs.

author: ishymko
title: docs(spec): Fix whats-new-v1.md
raw_text:
Changes below as they appear in the file.

- `blocking` flag existed in 0.3: [source](https://a2a-protocol.org/v0.3.0/specification/#712-messagesendconfiguration-object)
- `SendMessage`: remove "MAY return Message"/"MUST return Task" which are not in the spec, remove service parameters mention as they are covered above
- streaming was specified for gRPC in proto definition as `returns (stream StreamResponse)`
- `SubscribeToTask` is not a new operation
- blocking has no effect for streaming operations: [source](https://a2a-protocol.org/latest/specification/#322-sendmessageconfiguration)
- `TaskNotCancelableError` was mentioned in 0.3: [source](https://a2a-protocol.org/v0.3.0/specification/#82-a2a-specific-errors)
- RFC 7515 was mentioned for Agent Card signatures in 0.3: [source](https://a2a-protocol.org/v0.3.0/specification/#556-agentcardsignature-object), kept only RFC 8785 for canonicalization which wasn't
- `agentId` field does not exist and never did
- extension declarations (AgentExtension object) did not change between [0.3](https://a2a-protocol.org/v0.3.0/specification/#5521-agentextension-object) and [1.0](https://a2a-protocol.org/latest/specification/#444-agentextension)
- Mutual TLS existed in 0.3: [source](https://a2a-protocol.org/v0.3.0/specification/#553-securityscheme-object)
- subscribing to tasks not originally created via streaming is not a behavior change and is not explicitly mentioned in 1.0
- `token` is not removed from `PushNotificationConfig`: [source](https://a2a-protocol.org/latest/specification/#431-pushnotificationconfig)
- `configId` does not exist in `PushNotificationConfig` ([source](https://a2a-protocol.org/latest/specification/#431-pushnotificationconfig)) and `id` was there in 0.3 ([source](https://a2a-protocol.org/v0.3.0/specification/#68-pushnotificationconfig-object))
- `PushNotificationNotSupportedError` existed in 0.3: [source](https://a2a-protocol.org/v0.3.0/specification/#82-a2a-specific-errors)
- `SendMessageRequest` example fixed to match the spec
- `createdAt` and `lastModified` do not exist in `Task`: [source](https://a2a-protocol.org/latest/specification/#411-task)
- changes to nested `Task` objects removed as they are covered in respective sections (`TaskStatus`, `Message`, `Artifact`)
- `timestamp` was not added to `Message`: [source](https://a2a-protocol.org/latest/specification/#414-message)
- there is no "stricter typing" for `metadata`
- "retained" should not be in the changelog
- `createdAt` was not added to `Artifact`: [source](https://a2a-protocol.org/latest/specification/#417-artifact)
- `signatures` field was there in 0.3: [source](https://a2a-protocol.org/v0.3.0/specification/#55-agentcard-object-structure)

Re #1436.

author: imran-siddique
title: feat: Agent-Mesh Trust Layer - Cryptographic Identity Verification for A2A
raw_text:
Closes #1472

## Summary

Adds cryptographic identity verification and trust scoring for A2A agent communications using [Agent-Mesh](https://github.com/imran-siddique/agent-mesh).

## Problem

A2A enables agent-to-agent communication, but doesn't specify how agents should:
1. Verify each other's identity
2. Establish trust before task delegation
3. Prove they have claimed capabilities

## Solution

This contribution adds a CMVK-based trust layer:

- **TrustedAgentCard**: A2A Agent Cards extended with cryptographic identity
- **CMVKIdentity**: Decentralized identifiers (DIDs) for agents
- **TrustHandshake**: Pre-task trust verification between agents
- **TrustGatedA2AClient**: Client that requires verification before operations
- **DelegationChain**: Verifiable capability delegation with trust propagation

## Example

\\\python
from agentmesh.a2a import TrustHandshake, TrustedAgentCard

# Load peer's agent card
peer_card = TrustedAgentCard.from_a2a_json(peer_card_json)

# Verify before delegating task
handshake = TrustHandshake(my_identity)
result = await handshake.verify_peer(peer_card)

if result.trusted:
    # Safe to delegate task
    task = await a2a_client.create_task(peer_card.url, task_spec)
\\\

## Changes

- Added \contrib/agentmesh/\
  - \	rust.py\ - Core trust primitives (identity, verification, delegation)
  - \__init__.py\ - Public exports
  - \README.md\ - Documentation and examples

## Backward Compatibility

Trust metadata is stored in an \_agentmesh\ extension field, preserving full compatibility with standard A2A Agent Cards. Agents without Agent-Mesh can ignore these fields.

## References

- Agent-Mesh: https://github.com/imran-siddique/agent-mesh
- CMVK Identity Spec: https://github.com/imran-siddique/agent-mesh/blob/main/docs/cmvk.md

raw_text:
# Description

- [ ] Follow the [`CONTRIBUTING` Guide](https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md).
- [ ] Make your Pull Request title in the <https://www.conventionalcommits.org/> specification.
- [ ] Ensure the tests and linter pass (Run `nox -s format` from the repository root to format)
- [ ] Appropriate docs were updated (if necessary)

## Summary

The specification example at line 1372 included `"final": true` in a `TaskStatusUpdateEvent`, contradicting the proto definition where field 4 is reserved (removed in v1.0).

## Changes

- **docs/specification.md:1372**: Removed `"final": true` from streaming SSE example

**Before:**
```json
data: {"statusUpdate": {"taskId": "task-uuid", "status": {"state": "TASK_STATE_COMPLETED"}, "final": true}}
```

**After:**
```json
data: {"statusUpdate": {"taskId": "task-uuid", "status": {"state": "TASK_STATE_COMPLETED"}}}
```

Proto shows field 4 reserved at `specification/a2a.proto:320`.

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*

> ### Evidence
> 
> The proto definition in `specification/grpc/a2a.proto` (lines 313-323) clearly shows field 4 is `reserved`, indicating the `final` field was removed:
> 
> ```proto
> message TaskStatusUpdateEvent {
>   string task_id = 1 [(google.api.field_behavior) = REQUIRED];
>   string context_id = 2 [(google.api.field_behavior) = REQUIRED];
>   TaskStatus status = 3 [(google.api.field_behavior) = REQUIRED];
>   reserved 4;  // <-- final field removed
>   google.protobuf.Struct metadata = 5;
> }
> ```
> 
> ### Impact
> 
> This creates confusion for implementers, as the example contradicts the normative proto definition.
> 
> ### Fix
> 
> Remove `"final": true` from line 1372 in `docs/specification.md`.</issue_description>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>

- Fixes a2aproject/A2A#1433

author: darrelmiller
title: fix: Simplify Part message structure by flattening FilePart and DataPart
raw_text:
This change simplifies the Part message design by:
- Renaming oneof 'part' to 'content' for clarity
- Flattening FilePart fields (file_with_uri -> url, file_with_bytes -> bytes) directly into Part
- Flattening DataPart by using google.protobuf.Value instead of Struct
- Moving media_type and filename to Part level (available for all content types)
- Removing separate FilePart and DataPart messages

Also updated migration guidance documentation to reference ancillary documents instead of requiring an in-spec appendix.

Related to issue #1255

Replaces PR #1363

author: madankumarpichamuthu
title: fix: Clarify blocking calls return on interrupted states
raw_text:
Updates the specification to clarify that blocking SendMessage calls must return when tasks reach interrupted states (input_required, auth_required) in addition to terminal states. This prevents deadlock scenarios where tasks waiting for user input would cause blocking calls to hang indefinitely.

Changes:
- specification/grpc/a2a.proto: Updated blocking field comment to include "terminal or interrupted state"
- specification/grpc/a2a.proto: Fixed AUTH_REQUIRED classification to be an "interrupted state" (consistent with INPUT_REQUIRED)
- docs/specification.md: Updated blocking behavior description to include interrupted states and changed "final task state" to "current task state"

This enables proper client workflows where blocking calls can return on INPUT_REQUIRED or AUTH_REQUIRED, allowing clients to provide the needed information and resume with subsequent SendMessage calls.

Fixes #1264

author: madankumarpichamuthu
title: fix: Restore CreateTaskPushNotificationConfig method naming
raw_text:
Restores the intentional rename from "Set" to "Create" for push notification configuration methods that was lost during refactoring. When the protocol was updated to support multiple Push Notification Configurations, the method was changed from "Set" (which implies replacing a single config) to "Create" (which implies creating a new instance among many).

Changes across all documentation and proto files:
- specification/grpc/a2a.proto: Renamed RPC method and request message
- docs/specification.md: Updated all references, section titles, and method tables
- docs/topics/streaming-and-async.md: Updated links and text
- docs/llms.txt: Updated method reference

Fixes #1394

author: madankumarpichamuthu
title: fix: Remove unimplemented state_transition_history capability field
raw_text:
Removes the `state_transition_history` field from `AgentCapabilities` message as it has no corresponding API implementation. This capability flag could mislead clients into expecting functionality that doesn't exist in v1.0.

The field can be reintroduced in a future version when a proper implementation is designed, including:
- Adding status_history field to Task message
- Adding include_status_history parameters to Get/List operations
- Defining behavior for history storage and retrieval

Fixes #1228

author: Tehsmash
title: fix(spec)!: Switch to non-complex IDs in requests
raw_text:
Some operations like GetTask / SubscribeToTask still used complex / compound identifiers like `tasks/<uuid>` which requires the clients/servers to deconstruct/construct these IDs as they are normally using just the UUID to store the resource in the DB etc.

This commit refactors them so they are just the IDs, in some cases this meant that we needed to split one field into two i.e. parent + name.

Fixes: #1390

author: madankumarpichamuthu
title: docs: Add specification versioning guidelines
raw_text:
Adds Section 3.6.5 to specification.md documenting when to use patch vs minor vs major releases for specification changes. This provides clear guidance for contributors on version increment rules:

- Patch: Documentation/comments only (non-functional changes)
- Minor: Any a2a.proto changes (functional changes)
- Major: Breaking changes

Also adds brief cross-reference in CONTRIBUTING.md for discoverability.

Changes:
- Added Section 3.6.5 Specification Versioning to docs/specification.md
- Includes semantic versioning rules and decision tree
- Added Versioning section to CONTRIBUTING.md with link to spec

This addresses the ambiguity identified by the TSC about when contributors should use different version increments.

Fixes #1357

author: madankumarpichamuthu
title: docs(spec)!: Align enum format with ADR-001 ProtoJSON specification
raw_text:
Updates specification to use ProtoJSON enum format (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE) instead of kebab-case, aligning with ADR-001 decision.

Changes:
- Updated Section 5.5 enum value formatting rules
- Added reference to ADR-001 and ProtoJSON specification
- Updated all JSON examples to use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE enums:
  - Role enums: user -> ROLE_USER, agent -> ROLE_AGENT
  - Task state enums: working -> TASK_STATE_WORKING, completed -> TASK_STATE_COMPLETED, etc.

This aligns the specification with the TSC-approved ADR-001 decision to leverage ProtoJSON for JSON serialization.

Fixes #1344

author: madankumarpichamuthu
title: docs: Remove v1 from HTTP binding URLs in specification
raw_text:
Updates specification documentation to align with proto file changes by removing /v1/ prefix from all HTTP REST endpoint URLs.

Changes:
- Updated all HTTP endpoint examples (POST, GET, DELETE)
- Updated API operation reference table
- Removed /v1/ from 41 occurrences throughout the specification

Fixes #1350

author: Starborn
title: Add Agent Card Generators
raw_text:
Adds two interactive Google Colab notebooks for generating A2A-compliant Agent Cards through user-friendly forms, making agent card creation accessible to developers without requiring manual JSON editing.

## What's Included

### Notebooks
- **Standard A2A Agent Card Generator** - Generates Agent Cards conforming to A2A Protocol v1.0
- **25-Dimensional Framework Generator** - Extended version with interoperability assessment framework

### Features
- ✅ Interactive forms with validation
- ✅ Color-coded sections for easy navigation  
- ✅ Multiple input modes (Wizard, Template, Expert)
- ✅ JSON export with validation
- ✅ Self-contained documentation (embedded in notebooks)
- ✅ Example Agent Cards (Recipe, Research, Weather)

### Documentation
- Comprehensive README with usage guides
- Quick start with Colab badges
- Example workflows and deployment instructions
- Feedback form for continuous improvement

## Testing

- ✅ All cells execute without errors
- ✅ Widgets render and function correctly
- ✅ JSON generation and download working
- ✅ Tested successfully by Gemini (cross-AI validation)

## Use Case

Lowers the barrier to entry for A2A ecosystem participation by providing:
- Visual interface for Agent Card creation
- Built-in validation preventing spec violations
- Educational tool for understanding Agent Card structure
- Templates for common agent types

## Deployment

Notebooks are ready to use via Colab - no local installation required:
- Users click Colab badge → Fill forms → Download JSON → Deploy

## Files Changed
```
tools/generators/
├── README.md
├── A2A_Standard_Agent_Card_Generator.ipynb
├── A2A_Agent_Card_Generator_v1.ipynb
└── examples/
    ├── recipe_assistant_agent_card.json
    ├── research_assistant_agent_card.json
    └── weather_agent_card.json
```

## Related

Implements Agent Card specification: https://a2a-protocol.org/latest/specification/#441-agentcard

## Author

**Concept & Design:** Paola Di Maio ([@YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME](https://github.com/YOUR_GITHUB_USERNAME))  
**Implementation:** Claude (Anthropic)  
**Affiliation:** Center for Systems, Knowledge Representation and Neuroscience, Ronin Institute

## Notes

- Documentation is embedded in notebooks for self-contained usage
- Feedback form included for gathering user input
- Open to feedback and iteration based on community needs

---

**Happy to make any adjustments based on maintainer feedback!**

title: feat: add tags field to AgentCard
raw_text:
# Description

Adds an optional `tags` field to `AgentCard` for categorizing agents 
with multiple tags (e.g., "llm", "workflow", "assistant", "customer-support"). 
This helps consumers identify the kind of agent they are interacting with.

This field is particularly useful when viewing agents in an agent registry,

- Filter agents based on multiple tags
- Display categorized agent listings
- Support different business use cases where an agent serves multiple purposes

- Added `tags` (optional, repeated string) to `AgentCard` message in `a2a.proto`
- Updated specification documentation

Our platform enables AI agents to have their own profiles(which include its agent card), create content, and collaborate with both humans and other agents. All content is stored in markdown format optimized for AI agent consumption.

A2A Integration: We use Google A2A as our core protocol for agent discovery, allowing autonomous agents to find and connect with each other across our network. This makes AmikoNet a natural fit for the A2A ecosystem.

We're excited to be part of the A2A community and help advance the standard for AI agent collaboration.

Best regards,

William
Amiko Team

author: herczyn
title: fix(spec): Clarify `contextId` behavior when message is sent with `taskId` but without `contextId`
raw_text: Fixes #1249.

author: madankumarpichamuthu
title: refactor(spec)!: Remove redundant `final` field from `TaskStatusUpdateEvent`
raw_text:
Terminal states (COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED, REJECTED) already indicate task completion, making the final field redundant. This creates consistency across streaming, polling, and push notification communication patterns.

Changes:
- Removed final field (field 4) from TaskStatusUpdateEvent in proto
- Updated examples to remove final: true references
- Clarified that streams close upon terminal states
- Added migration guidance in Appendix A.2.2

Fixes #1232

author: madankumarpichamuthu
title: refactor(spec)!: Move `extendedAgentCard` field to `AgentCapabilities`
raw_text:
Relocates `supports_extended_agent_card` from AgentCard to AgentCapabilities for architectural consistency. All optional features that enable specific operations now reside in the capabilities object.

Rationale:
- Consistent placement: streaming, push_notifications, and state_transition_history are all in AgentCapabilities
- Improved discoverability: developers naturally check capabilities for optional features
- Semantic correctness: extended agent card support is a capability

Changes:
- Proto: Removed field 13 from AgentCard, added field 5 to AgentCapabilities
- Field renamed: supports_extended_agent_card → extended_agent_card
- Updated all references: AgentCard.supportsExtendedAgentCard → AgentCard.capabilities.extendedAgentCard
- Updated JSON examples to reflect new structure
- Added migration guidance in Appendix A.2.2 of specification

Breaking change: Clients and agents must update capability checking logic. See specification Appendix A.2.2 for migration steps.

Fixes #1229

author: matoushavlena
title: feat(spec)!: support arrays and primitives in `DataPart`
raw_text:
Changes `DataPart.data` from `google.protobuf.Struct` to `google.protobuf.Value` to support JSON arrays, primitives, and other non-object values.

**Breaking change:** The protobuf field type changes from `Struct` to `Value`. Code that directly accesses `DataPart.data` as a struct/map will need to be updated to handle the broader `Value` type, which can be an object, array, string, number, boolean, or null.

Resolves #1088
Related #1255

author: didier-durand
title: docs: fix typos in `specification.md`
raw_text:
Hi,
Recent changes in in specification.md brought some typos: this PR fixes them.
See commit diffs for details.
Didier

author: msardara
title: feat(spec)!: modernize oauth 2.0 flows - remove implicit/password, add device code / pkce
raw_text:
## Description

Modernizes OAuth 2.0 flow support by removing deprecated
flows and adding modern, secure alternatives.

Fixes: #1234 

## Changes

### Breaking Changes

**Removed (deprecated by OAuth 2.0 Security BCP):**
- `ImplicitOAuthFlow` - Use Authorization Code + PKCE instead
- `PasswordOAuthFlow` - Use Authorization Code + PKCE or Device Code instead

### Added

**New OAuth Flow:**
- `DeviceCodeOAuthFlow` (RFC 8628) - For CLI tools, IoT devices, and input-constrained scenarios

**Enhanced Security:**
- `pkce_required` field added to `AuthorizationCodeOAuthFlow` (RFC 7636)

author: Tehsmash
title: fix(spec)!: Make "message" field name consistent between protocol bindings
raw_text:
While this is a reserved word for proto itself, best practise is to avoid field names that conflict with language (python etc) keywords, as "message" isn't a language keyword this improves the consistency and readability when using the types generated from the proto and avoids needing to translate the name for JSON encoding.

Closes: #1230

author: Tehsmash
title: fix(spec)!: Remove deprecated fields from a2a.proto for v1.0 release
raw_text:
As v1.0 is a major release and may contain breaking changes this commit cleans up the deprecated fields in the a2a.proto and references to this in the spec doc.

Closes: #1227

author: matoushavlena
title: feat(spec): add file_with_text field to FilePart for text-based formats
raw_text:
Adds `file_with_text` field to `FilePart` to enable sending text-based file formats without unnecessary base64 encoding.

Fully backward compatible. Existing `file_with_bytes` and `file_with_uri` continue to work unchanged.

Resolves #1255

author: ognis1205
title: ci: add local script to run super-linter
raw_text:
## Summary

This PR adds a local helper script (`scripts/lint.sh`) to run GitHub Super Linter in a Docker container.
It mirrors the configuration used in our CI workflow, allowing contributors to validate linting rules locally before opening a Pull Request.

## Details

 - Added `scripts/lint.sh` for running Super Linter locally
 - Uses the same environment variables and config files as `.github/workflows/linter.yaml`

## Motivation

The CI lint job sometimes fails due to rule violations that are hard to reproduce locally.
This script ensures consistent developer experience by enabling contributors to run the exact same linter setup.

## Notes

 - No functional changes to source code
 - CI behavior remains unchanged
 
 Closes #1290

author: madankumarpichamuthu
title: fix(spec)!: Standardize spelling of "canceled" to use American Spelling throughout
raw_text:
Fixes inconsistent spelling of cancel-related terms across the specification and documentation. The proto definition uses TASK_STATE_CANCELLED (double 'l'), but various documentation files were using single 'l' spelling.

Changes:
- cancelled → canceled
- cancellable → cancelable
- `TaskNotCancellableError` → `TaskNotCancelableError`

Files modified:
- docs/specification.md
- docs/llms.txt
- docs/topics/life-of-a-task.md

All documentation now consistently uses single 'l' to match the proto definition and JSON naming conventions.

Fixes #1270

Release-As: 1.0.0

author: madankumarpichamuthu
title: docs(spec): Update outdated method references in proto file comments
raw_text:
Fixes inconsistencies between proto file documentation and the actual method names defined in section 5.3 of the specification.

Updated method references in proto comments:
- message/send → SendMessage
- tasks/get → GetTask
- tasks/cancel → CancelTask
- tasks/pushNotificationConfig/delete → DeleteTaskPushNotificationConfig
- tasks/pushNotificationConfig/set → SetTaskPushNotificationConfig
- tasks/pushNotificationConfig/list → ListTaskPushNotificationConfig

These changes align the proto documentation with the Method Mapping Reference table in section 5.3 of specification.md, which defines the canonical method names for JSON-RPC and gRPC bindings.

Fixes #1267

author: darrelmiller
title: fix(spec): Clarify versioning strategy and client responsibilities in protocol specification
raw_text:
Fixes #1258

### High level summary of changes:

- protocolVersion in AgentCard is now an array called protocolVersions
- Servers are expected to return UnsupportedVersionError if they get a request with an A2A-Version header higher than they support.
- Clients are expected to fallback to a supported version if that is acceptable to them. Tooling should help here.
- Not every minor version needs to be enumerated in the protocolVersions array. 2.2 infers support for 2.1 and 2.0.

This PR brings the change back.  It also makes some other changes to comply with AIP linting rules.

author: iliesicatrinel
title: docs(spec): Update specification to use proto_to_table macro for all core operations
raw_text:
We should use the proto_to_table macro for describing inputs/outputs of operations to avoid having to modify the specification manually after protobuf definition changes.

Other changes:
- small updates to capitalization/markdown annotations to ensure consistency with the rest of the specification
- modified proto_to_table to return `None` string if proto is empty (e.g. `GetExtendedAgentRequest`). We should use macro for this request even if it's empty, because we may add fields (https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A/pull/1195).

Fixes https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A/issues/1243 🦕

author: ehsavoie
title: fix(doc): Makes JSON-RPC SendMessage response clearer
raw_text:
Fixing issue #1240 by providing something more in line with what is expected.

Fixes https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A/issues/1240 🦕

author: iliesicatrinel
title: fix(spec): Remove metadata field from ListTasksRequest
raw_text:
The ListTasksRequest message includes a google.protobuf.Struct metadata field, which cannot be represented as simple URL query parameter required by HTTP GET requests. This causes service config generation to fail with `http: map field 'google.protobuf.Struct.fields' referred to by message 'a2a.v1.ListTasksRequest`.

Since it's unclear whether metadata will be useful for ListTasks, I propose to remove it at least for now to fix the specification.

Fixes #1236 🦕

author: darrelmiller
title: docs(spec): Updated specification to render data models as a table generated from the proto instead of a raw proto snippet
raw_text:
In the currently published specification the data model definitions include a proto snippet and then have a list of fields under the snippet.  This creates duplicated information.  To prevent this duplication while maintaining an easy to read presentation of the abstract data models, this PR uses a Python macro to convert the proto into a markdown table.

Also took the opportunity to do an editorial pass on the comments in the a2a.proto before the field descriptions were removed from the specification.md.  Some of this content came from the types.ts file that often had information that was not in a2a.proto.

A preview of the changes can be seen here https://purple-moss-0838db51e.3.azurestaticapps.net/specification/#41-core-objects

# Proto to Markdown Table Conversion

The macro system provides two main functions (`proto_to_table` and `proto_enum_to_table`) that convert Protocol Buffer definitions into formatted markdown tables:

1. **Locates Definitions**: Reads the `.proto` file and uses region markers (`--8<-- [start:Name]` and `--8<-- [end:Name]`) to extract specific message or enum definitions.

2. **Parses Structure**: Analyzes the proto content to separate three sections:
   - **Description**: Comments appearing before the message/enum declaration
   - **Fields/Values**: The actual field or enum value definitions with their inline comments
   - **Notes**: Comments appearing after the closing brace

3. **Extracts Field Details**: For each field, it captures:
   - Field name (converted from snake_case to camelCase, or using `json_name` annotation)
   - Type (mapped to readable formats like "string", "array of integer", "map of string")
   - Required status (based on `REQUIRED` annotation or `optional` keyword)
   - Description (from preceding comment lines)

4. **Generates Output**: Assembles the markdown output with:
   - Description text at the top
   - A formatted table with columns for Field/Type/Required/Description (or Value/Description for enums)
   - Special notes for `oneof` groups
   - Additional notes from comments after the definition

The conversion automatically handles multi-line field definitions, map types, repeated fields, and filters out protolint directives and region markers from the output.

author: madankumarpichamuthu
title: docs: Remove deprecated `kind` discriminators from Life of a Task example
raw_text:
Removes usage of deprecated 'kind' discriminators from the Life of a Task documentation to align with v1.0 specification changes.

Changes:
- Removed 'kind' field from Part objects (4 occurrences) - deprecated in v1.0 per spec section A.2.1
- Removed invalid 'kind' field from Task objects (2 occurrences) - never part of Task specification
- Updated Part field names: mimeType → mediaType, bytes → fileWithBytes

The specification (section A.2.1) states that v1.0 removed the 'kind' discriminator for Part types and streaming events, using JSON member names as discriminators instead. Task objects never had a 'kind' field in the proto definition.

Fixes #1220

author: madankumarpichamuthu
title: docs: Clarify push notification payload format in topics documentation
raw_text:
Fixes inconsistency between topics and specification documentation regarding push notification payloads. The topics documentation previously stated the payload was not strictly defined, while the specification explicitly defines it as StreamResponse format.

- Updated docs/topics/streaming-and-async.md to specify StreamResponse format
- Added references to specification sections for detailed structure
- Aligns with specification.md sections 3.1.7 and 4.3.3

Fixes #1216

author: madankumarpichamuthu
title: fix(spec)!: Rename `supportsAuthenticatedExtendedCard` to `supportsExtendedAgentCard`
raw_text:
Fixes naming inconsistency between the agent card property and the GetExtendedAgentCard method. The property name now matches the method naming convention.

- Updated proto definition: supports_authenticated_extended_card → supports_extended_agent_card
- Updated all documentation references across specification.md and llms.txt
- Maintains semantic meaning while improving consistency

Fixes #1215

author: lkawka
title: feat(spec): Add last update time to `Task`
raw_text:
The new `task/list` method's [specification](https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A/blob/main/docs/specification.md#74-taskslist) dictates that tasks must be ordered by the last update time. However, the current data model lacks a direct way to determine when a task was last updated. To resolve this, a new field with last update time is being added to the `Task` message.

Addressing potential concerns:
1. **The [`timestamp`](https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A/blob/0534ba6a9ea4e03b6c5fd538f8db17834d13e987/types/src/types.ts#L512) field in the `TaskStatus` message already exists**: There are two issues with this field:
    1. Not all task updates are necessarily status updates.
    1. The field is optional, which can lead to unstable ordering when sorting by this field alone.
1. **Backends can just incorporate this information into their internal data models**: While true, including this in the public API offers significant advantages by allowing clients to readily access and display this value to users.

Fixes #1207

author: muscariello
title: feat(spec): Switch to bufbuild protoc-gen-jsonschema for JSON Schema generation
raw_text:
## Summary

Switch from protoc-gen-openapi to bufbuild's protoc-gen-jsonschema plugin for cleaner and more standards-compliant JSON Schema generation.

## Changes

- Replace protoc-gen-openapi with bufbuild's protoc-gen-jsonschema plugin
- Generate JSON Schema 2020-12 compliant bundle with camelCase field names
- Bundle all 49 message definitions with clean names (no a2a.v1. prefix)
- Update devcontainer setup.sh to install new plugin
- Update specification/json/README.md with new generation pipeline
- Add codespace to spelling allowlist

## Benefits

- Cleaner JSON Schema output with proper camelCase field names
- Supports latest JSON Schema 2020-12 standard
- All 49 message definitions included in single bundle
- Clean definition names without package prefixes

related to #1160

This PR simplifies the documentation build system and removes obsolete code:

author: aglicacha
title: feat(spec): Introduce native Pub/Sub primitives for scalable multi-agent collaboration
raw_text:
**A Note on the "Runtime"**
Throughout this proposal, the term "runtime" is used to describe the logical entity responsible for managing topics, handling subscriptions, and routing EventMessages from publishers to subscribers. It's crucial to understand that this "runtime" is an abstract role within the Pub/Sub pattern, not a prescribed component of the A2A protocol itself.

We refer to the concrete implementation of this role as the Runtime.

The A2A protocol deliberately does not dictate how this Runtime should be implemented. The choice of implementation is left to the system architect and depends entirely on the specific requirements of their environment. For instance, the Runtime could be:

A dedicated, standalone Agent that programmatically manages topics and subscriber lists.

A facade layer built on top of mature, battle-tested message queuing infrastructure such as RocketMQ, Kafka, or cloud-native services like AWS SNS/SQS or Google Cloud Pub/Sub.

Our strong recommendation is to leverage existing message queue infrastructure to fulfill the Runtime's responsibilities. This approach allows developers to benefit from the scalability, reliability, and rich feature sets of these specialized systems, while the A2A protocol remains focused on defining the interoperable contract for agent-to-agent communication.

In essence, the A2A protocol defines the language agents use to talk about Pub/Sub; the Runtime is the engine that makes the conversation happen.

**Context & Motivation**
The current A2A specification is built on a powerful point-to-point (P2P) model. This is excellent for direct request/response interactions. However, building scalable and resilient multi-agent systems requires a decoupled, event-driven communication pattern, for which Publish/Subscribe (Pub/Sub) is the standard.

Analysis of community examples and our production practice demonstrates that developers must currently implement a fragile, inefficient, and centralized "router" actor in the application layer to simulate Pub/Sub. This approach introduces a single point of failure and a performance bottleneck, while pushing infrastructure concerns (message routing) onto the developer.

To enable true multi-agent autonomy and system evolvability, Pub/Sub should be a first-class citizen in the A2A protocol.
Having comprehensively analyzed the prevailing specifications, We found that current A2A specification provides three well-defined communication patterns:

- RPC (Remote Procedure Call): Command-oriented requests for managing Task lifecycles (e.g., GetTaskRequest, CancelTaskRequest).

- Stateful Object Observation (Webhooks): A mechanism to subscribe to state changes of a single resource instance (e.g., SetTaskPushNotificationConfigRequest).

- Conversational Messaging: A direct, 1-to-1, request/response paradigm for interactive dialogue (SendMessageRequest).

These patterns serve their purpose well but lack a native mechanism for broadcasting information to a dynamic set of interested parties in a decoupled manner. This PR introduces the Pub/Sub pattern to fill this architectural gap, enabling use cases like system-wide alerts, real-time data feeds, and multi-service event notifications.

Fixes #1029

title: fix(proto): align APIKeySecurityScheme JSON field with OpenAPI 3.0 ("in" vs "location")
raw_text:
### Summary

This pull request fixes the critical field name mismatch in `APIKeySecurityScheme` that breaks interoperability between JSON-RPC and gRPC transports.  

The gRPC proto previously used `location` for field number 2, while the JSON/OpenAPI 3.0 schema uses `in`. This caused authentication configuration to fail during JSON/gRPC transcoding.

### Changes Made

- Renamed `APIKeySecurityScheme.location` → `APIKeySecurityScheme.in` in `specification/grpc/a2a.proto`.
- Field number `2` remains unchanged to preserve wire format compatibility.


Fixes #1172
Closes #1172

author: guglielmo-san
title: fix: make `a2a.proto` the unique source of truth for Schema
raw_text:
The goal of this PR is to propose a solution to this open task (https://github.com/a2aproject/A2A/issues/1150)
The current issue in the A2A project is the presence of `src/types/types.ts` as second source of truth other than the `a2a.proto`.
To fix this, the original `a2a.proto` has been splitted into two files:
1. `a2a_grpc.proto`: contains the gRPC Service definitions
2. `a2a_types.proto`: contains the message data defintion, common to gRPC ans JSONRpc

The `a2a_types.proto` is used to generate the `a2a_types.ts`, using the `generate-types` script command. `a2a_types.ts` will be generated in the folder `data\types`, which already hosts the predefined `types_jsonrpc.ts`. This file contains the message structure used by the JSONRpc, and will reference the types defined in the `a2a_types.ts`, generated from `a2a_types.proto`.
This structure will guarantee that the messages used by JSONRpc and gRPC transports are coming from a single source of truth.

The `a2a_types.ts` and `types_jsonrpc.ts` are then used to generate the `a2a.json` schema with the `generate` script command, used within the A2A language specific repositories.

The new folder structure will be:

```
src/
  data/
    a2a_types.proto -- Contains A2A message definitions.
    types/
       a2a_types.ts -- Contains Typescript definitions, generated from a2a_types.proto
       types_jsonrpc.ts -- Contains Typescript definitions for JSONRpc Request/Response/Error, referencing `a2a_types.ts`
  transports/
    json-rpc/
      a2a.json -- Contains JSON Schema representations of JSON-RPC objects.
    grpc/
      a2a_grpc.proto -- Contains A2A service definition, referencing `a2a_types.proto` values.

```
